steps.title: Use the overlook clue
steps.content: The second sentence says scholars may "inadvertently overlook nuances" when they focus only on how an author fits a movement. The blank must therefore mean to treat subtleties as unimportant or fail to give them proper attention.

steps.title: Separate opposite meanings
steps.content: Choice A means to enlarge or emphasize subtleties, and Choice B means to examine them closely; both oppose the clue. Choice D means to publicly condemn, which is too strong and not the same as overlooking. The remaining word means to disregard. The answer is C.
